steps.title: Read the table values
steps.content: From the table: Greater than 65 years old is 21%. The ages at least 18 include: 18-40 (23%), 41-65 (22%), and Greater than 65 (21%), totaling 66%.

steps.title: Apply conditional probability formula
steps.content:
We need P(greater than 65 | at least 18). Since everyone over 65 is automatically at least 18, this equals the proportion over 65 divided by the proportion at least 18:
$$P = \frac{21\%}{66\%} \approx 0.318$$
This is closest to 0.32, matching choice B — B is correct.
